title: quercus × mannifera
intro: Quercus × mannifera, also known as the Manchineel tree, is a hybrid plant that belongs to the Quercus genus, combining the characteristics of both Quercus robur and Quercus cerris. The bark, leaves, and sap of the Quercus × mannifera plant have been used in traditional medicine for their various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus × mannifera include its potential to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and protect against infections, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. The plant is typically harvested for its herbal preparations, such as decoctions, infusions, and salves, which are used to treat a range of ailments from skin conditions to respiratory issues.
